name: Phanuel
description: Phanuel, whose name signifies "Face of God" or "Vision of God," is a prominent angel within the Abrahamic traditions, particularly in Jewish apocalyptic literature and Christian angelology. He is generally categorized as one of the four angels of the Presence, standing before God's throne alongside Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael. Phanuel is associated with judgment, repentance, hope, and intercession. Some texts depict him as the angel who guards the gate of Paradise or as a leader of angelic worship. His role as an interpreter of divine visions and a guide for those seeking spiritual understanding is also highlighted. While not as universally recognized as some other archangels, Phanuel's significance lies in his representation of divine justice, mercy, and the transformative power of encountering the divine presence. He embodies the concept of seeing God's face and being changed by the experience, encouraging spiritual growth and repentance.
subtitle: Face of God

keyMyths.title: Phanuel and the Book of Enoch
keyMyths.description: In the Book of Enoch, Phanuel is described as one of the four archangels who stand before God's throne, responsible for expelling the evil spirits and preventing them from ascending to heaven and accusing humanity. He is described as the angel of repentance, whose voice is heard throughout the celestial realms, calling for repentance and renewal. This role highlights his function as a mediator between humanity and the divine, offering a path to redemption through repentance.

keyMyths.title: Phanuel and the Vision of Judgment
keyMyths.description: According to some interpretations, Phanuel is associated with the final judgment, particularly in the context of separating the righteous from the unrighteous. He is sometimes depicted as wielding a fiery sword, symbolizing divine justice and the consequences of sin. This role underscores his connection to divine order and the importance of living a life in accordance with God's will, as he is a visual reminder of the accountability that awaits all souls.

keyMyths.title: Phanuel as Guardian of Paradise
keyMyths.description: Some traditions identify Phanuel as one of the angels guarding the gates of Paradise. This role reflects his association with purity, righteousness, and the protection of sacred spaces. As a guardian, he ensures that only those who are worthy may enter the divine presence. This myth highlights Phanuel's role as a gatekeeper, representing the challenges and requirements for achieving spiritual enlightenment and communion with God.
